mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines yield positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances during high inflation, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL growth falls below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate compression that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread fails to predict 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive returns occur in > 55% of cases when the spread narrows instead of widens.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage income, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or negative returns dominate (> 55% of cases) during periods of rapid employment expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product signals an economic contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of quarters following a GDP decline, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) when GDP contractions are mild (< 0.5%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a synchronized supply and labor recovery that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during post-shock recoveries.
failure_condition: The combined supply-labor improvement fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism breaks down when inflation exceeds 4%.
